articles.title: Zambia cancels world’s largest human rights and tech summit days before start
articles.description:
Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’
The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”.
Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May, but Thabo Kawana, permanent secretary for the Ministry of Information & Media, said last week that the conference would not go ahead to allow time to ensure the gathering “aligns with Zambia’s national values, policy priorities, and broader public interest considerations”.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Blocking stress signals could limit harmful inflammation after heart attack
articles.description: After a heart attack, the body rapidly floods the injured heart with neutrophils—white blood cells that help repair damage but can also make it worse when too many arrive too quickly. New research from the University of Oklahoma shows that these early-arriving cells come not from the bone marrow, as long believed, but from a hidden reserve along blood vessel walls. The discovery also identifies a potential way to limit their harmful surge.

articles.title: First malaria drug for babies is approved in ‘major public health milestone’
articles.description:
WHO prequalification of Coartem Baby means newborns can be safely treated rather than using medication for older children
The first malaria treatment for babies has been approved by the World Health Organization, opening the door to widespread use around the globe.
In parts of Africa, up to 18% of children under six months will be infected with malaria, but there has historically been no safe treatment for the smallest of them. There were 610,000 deaths from malaria in 2024, about three quarters of which were under-fives in Africa.

articles.title: Angel Reese takes on her old team and Queen Camilla puts the finishing touches on a library's Pooh collection

articles.title: Nagaland to mark World Red Cross Day with 'Walk for Humanity' on May 8
articles.description: Nagaland to mark World Red Cross Day with 'Walk for Humanity' on May 8

articles.title: Census operations director in U.P. says exercise to include caste enumeration
articles.description: In the context of population, if you are ordinarily residing in a particular location, you are considered part of that area's population and will be enumerated, says the director of census operations in the State, Sheetal Verma

articles.title: Air India CEO search narrows to Singapore Air executive Kannan and insider Nipun Aggarwal, sources say
articles.description: The choice of a new CEO comes at a crucial time for Air India, which ⁠is grappling with heavy losses, heightened regulatory scrutiny after a deadly crash last year, and operational disruptions from the Iran war, ‌which has driven up costs

articles.title: SC rejects TMC plea against EC counting officer order for West Bengal polls
articles.description: Supreme Court refuses to hear TMC plea against EC order mandating Central govt officers at counting tables, upholding poll panel’s discretion.

articles.title: Dan Bongino reveals he fears ‘federal prison’ in chilling claim after year at the FBI
articles.description: Former Deputy FBI Director Dan Bongino says he uncovered Russiagate files in FBI burn bags and now fears retribution for reforming the Bureau.

articles.title: Democrats who rallied at ‘No Kings’ protests applaud King Charles in Congress
articles.description: Democrats face mockery for welcoming King Charles III to Congress after rallying at 'No Kings' protests targeting presidential power weeks earlier.

topStories.title: Melania's White House beehive and gifts of honey strike sweet note during King Charles' royal visit
topStories.description: From a hive tour on White House grounds to a honey-themed dessert, Melania Trump's beekeeping program took center stage during King Charles' and Queen Camilla's royal visit.

topStories.title: 'Needs to resign': California vice mayor ripped after commenting '86 47' on state GOP's online post
topStories.description: California GOP demands Los Altos Vice Mayor Larry Lang resign over "86 47" posts he made on social media roughly a month after ex-FBI Director James Comey was indicted for the same thing.

topStories.title: Americans continue voting with their feet as high-tax cities struggle to recover
topStories.description: NYC and LA lose residents as taxpayers "vote with their feet" for the Southeast. Discover why states like Florida and South Carolina are booming.

topStories.title: Asal Sayas died of colon cancer at 42. She advocated for patients until the end.
topStories.description: The daughter of Iranian immigrants, she worked in the White House and Senate, lobbied for AIDS research and became a tireless champion for people with cancer.

topStories.title: Operation Neptune Spear: Killing bin Laden | 60 Minutes Archive
topStories.description: In 2012, Scott Pelley spoke with a retired Navy SEAL, and former member of SEAL Team 6, who recounted the May 2011 raid on Osama bin Laden's compound in Abbottabad, Pakistan.

topStories.title: Boy, 14, shot dead by Israeli settlers in West Bank amid escalation in violence
topStories.description: AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead

topStories.title: Supreme Court ruling on counting staff for Bengal polls crumbled TMC's bid to seize power unfairly: BJP
topStories.description: BJP national spokesperson claimed that the Trinamool approached various courts more than 80 times in the past 10-12 years by “misusing constitutional instruments”

topStories.description: Talks with bondholders for a government bailout this week failed to yield a deal.

dailyNews.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
dailyNews.description: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.

dailyNews.title: US-Iran conflict: Tehran issues new maritime rules to tighten authority over Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said the force will exercise ‘control over nearly 2,000 km (1,243 miles) of Iran’s coastline’ spanning the Arabian Gulf and the vital Strait of Hormuz

dailyNews.title: No Breakthrough: Trump Rules Out Early End to War, Iran Holds Firm On Nuclear, Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Pertinently, Today is the deadline for the Trump administration to request legal authorisation, since the Iran conflict began 60 days ago.

dailyNews.title: FDA expands access to drug helping pancreatic cancer patients
dailyNews.description: Former Sen. Ben Sasse, 54, called daraxonrasib "a miracle drug" that was allowing him to live longer and with less pain.

dailyNews.title: Trump is running out of options to contain gas price backlash
dailyNews.description: The rising cost of oil is becoming a major political concern for the White House, as the stalemate between President Donald Trump and Iran drags on.

dailyNews.title: A defiant Iran leaves Trump with few options
dailyNews.description: A legal deadline for congressional authorization threatens to increase pressure on the administration — and underscore lagging support for the unpopular war.

dailyNews.title: Trump to downsize U.S. military presence in Germany
dailyNews.description: Germany said the U.S. decision was “anticipated” while NATO said it was working to understand the details of the plan, which coincides with a feud between the president and the German chancellor.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Trump tells Congress hostilities 'have terminated'
dailyNews.description: Despite facing the 60-day deadline under the War Powers Act, the president is not asking Congress for authorization.

dailyNews.title: US threatens sanctions if shipping firms pay Iran fees for Strait of Hormuz passage
dailyNews.description: The US has warned shipping companies against making payments to Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, citing sanctions risks. This alert comes amid rising tensions and disruptions to global oil trade, with Iran rerouting vessels and charging fees. The US has also imposed a naval blockade, further straining energy markets and complicating diplomatic efforts.

dailyNews.title: After Assad's fall, Syria's Kurds are left in limbo, feeling abandoned by the U.S.
dailyNews.description: Caught in limbo after the fall of Syria's Bashar al-Assad, Kurdish families struggle with cold, loss and uncertainty — feeling abandoned by the U.S. allies they once fought alongside.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Vision for D.C. ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statues Grows in Size and Cost
dailyNews.description: Plans for the project include formal gardens, reflecting pools and plazas alongside the statues of 250 notable Americans, according to documents obtained by The New York Times.

dailyNews.title: These G.O.P. Women Want Congress Members to Pay a Price for Abuse. Will It Work?
dailyNews.description: Three Republican women who had a hand in forcing the resignations of lawmakers accused of sexual misconduct said they are out to name and shame more offenders. It is not clear how far they will go.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after White House rescue deal falls through
dailyNews.description: The airline said early Saturday it had “no choice” but to wind down operations immediately, dashing hopes of a last-minute financial lifeline.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after rescue talks collapse
dailyNews.description: The airline had been in talks with the Trump administration about a $500m bailout.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after failed effort at government rescue deal
dailyNews.description: The budget carrier Spirit Airlines is ceasing operations after failing to land a $500 million bailout from the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations after escalating financial struggles
dailyNews.description: The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Shuts Operations After White House Bailout Falls Apart
dailyNews.description: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. has started an orderly wind-down of operations, the troubled discount carrier said in a statement Saturday.

dailyNews.title: What to know about Spirit Airlines amid reports airline preparing to cease operations
dailyNews.description: The Wall Street Journal reported Spirit Airlines will cease operations after a rescue deal from the White House apparently fell through.

dailyNews.title: Secret Service 'model worked' during WHCA Dinner shooting but 'luck' played a role, experts say
dailyNews.description: Questions swirl over Secret Service security after an armed gunman allegedly attempted to assassinate Trump at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner.

dailyNews.title: Trump teases US will be 'taking over' Cuba 'almost immediately' in Florida speech
dailyNews.description: Speaking at a Palm Beach forum, Trump appeared to joke the U.S. would be “taking over" Cuba “almost immediately" and that he'll "finish a job" in Iran first.

dailyNews.title: Pentagon orders withdrawal of 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as Trump escalates feud with Merz
dailyNews.description: The Pentagon confirmed the withdrawal of about 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as tensions between Trump and Chancellor Merz intensify.

dailyNews.title: N.I.H. Reinstates Employee Put on Leave After Criticizing Trump Research Cuts
dailyNews.description: Jenna Norton had filed a whistle-blower complaint claiming that the agency leadership had retaliated against her.

dailyNews.title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany, Pentagon Says
dailyNews.description: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.

dailyNews.title: How Germany May Have Misjudged Trump’s Anger on Iran
dailyNews.description: After Chancellor Friedrich Merz upset President Trump with criticism of the war, he offered no public sign he believed Mr. Trump’s threats to pull troops were serious.
